pages.full_text:
some shopping. Eve read + wrote post cards. William read. John + Charles played with the children near the lake.
    In the afternoon after much waiting around, Simon, William + John went off in one row boat + Peter, Eve + Charles went in another. We rowed through our section of the lake to another, while another, still bigger, lay beyond.
    After cakes in the middle, Simon + Eve swopped boats, + Simon beat [strikethrough] John + William
rowing back, by rejecting our paddle!

[Hand-drawn sketch of a standing figure]
Resurrection Panel, Figure More Ni Bhrien or [?figure] of Wisdom on MacMahon tomb; influenced by Nottingham alabaster carvings 1470 Ennis Friary
photo_captions: Resurrection Panel, Figure More Ni Bhrien or [?figure] of Wisdom on MacMahon tomb; influenced by Nottingham alabaster carvings 1470 Ennis Friary
pages.notes: This page contains a hand-drawn sketch of a standing figure (likely copied from the Resurrection Panel at Ennis Friary, Co. Clare, Ireland). The text is a continuation from the previous page — the narrative is mid-sentence at top ('some shopping'). The sketch caption mentions 'Figure More Ni Bhrien' which may be 'Mor Ni Bhriain' (anglicised: More Ni Bhrien). The strikethrough over 'sat.' before 'John' is visible in original. The page number is not visible in the top-left corner.

pages.full_text: Clonmacnoise, Co Offaly  Cross of the Scriptures.
photo_captions: Clonmacnoise, Co Offaly  Cross of the Scriptures.
pages.notes: This page is dominated by a glued-in postcard/photo of the Cross of the Scriptures at Clonmacnoise, County Offaly, Ireland. The only handwritten text is the caption below the photo. There appears to be a partial page number at the very top right of the page but it is cut off and illegible. The left-hand margin shows the bleed-through of the left page text.
